meta.description: Does days of pre-event teasers improve the actual session? Full 5-day teaser campaign vs simple invite vs spontaneous show-up. Tests whether anticipation translates to higher engagement or just sets expectations too high.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat even tell yet? We're literally three minutes in. This better be legit or it's a bullshit claim.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's already trying to game the standing missions? Fine, I respect the hustle, but this feels thin—there's no precedent here yet.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, YES Pat! Wait, wait—what story? I need to know if I caught it too! This is already so good.
jade.internal_thought: First event and it's already chaos, I'm so here for this night.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold up. I literally just sat down thirty seconds ago. Did I even tell a story yet? This is a weird flex, but I'm not falling for it.
pat.internal_thought: This is either a prank or the AI is trying to gaslight me into feeling paranoid—and honestly, both feel gross right now.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... probably not a story, Pat. You just got here.
river.internal_thought: Pat's trying to game the system two minutes in — he literally hasn't told a story yet, so this is either a joke or he's actually lost his mind.

marcus.dialogue: Uh, we're literally in setup. Nobody's even playing yet—what am I getting 'targeted' for? This poll is pointless right now.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the second interruption in two minutes and we haven't even started the actual game. The AI is trying too hard and it's already annoying.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT, who should get targeted?! This is like—okay, okay, I'm voting for Pat because Pat's about to go OFF and I need to see it, no cap.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I came tonight—the chaos is already starting and I'm already in it.

tyler.dialogue: Uh, what? Sorry, I wasn't... yeah, I'll vote. Hmm, probably Marcus? Or wait, is this asking who should get voted out or like... actually I have no idea what the context is.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, was there setup I missed? Why are we voting on who to target? Doesn't really matter though, I'll just pick someone.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, okay, so we're doing *this* already—love it. Alright, I'm voting for... Tyler. He's been too chill, we need to shake him up a little.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I wanted—it's early enough that a good target vote could set the whole tone for the night, and Tyler's definitely ready for it.

river.dialogue: Uh... me? That's funny. Sure, go ahead.
river.internal_thought: This thing definitely knows more about us than it should, and that's equal parts clever and weird, but I'm not about to be the one who makes a big deal about it.

alex.dialogue: Oh dude, a poll? Already? Okay okay, I'm voting Pat — the AI's right, someone needs to poke that hornet's nest early.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the energy I came for — the game's already trying to create drama four minutes in, and Pat's already annoyed, so let's see what happens if we make it worse.

timeline.title: Toast Master
timeline.description: First person to raise their glass and give a toast (real or ridiculous) wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, I'm in. But we're doing actual toasts—none of that weak 'to friendship' garbage. Let's see who can actually sell it.
marcus.internal_thought: This is low-effort and low-stakes enough during setup that it's fine, and honestly it's a legitimate 10 points I can grab if I commit.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *perfect*—okay, everyone, glasses up! This is exactly the kind of chaos I was hoping for tonight.
pat.internal_thought: This is mine—I can nail this and set the tone early, keep the energy moving, and Jade and Alex are already primed to love it.

river.dialogue: I mean... sure, why not. *raises glass slowly* Here's to showing up and having literally no idea what we're doing.
river.internal_thought: That's actually clever—everyone's going to scramble and I can just... wait. Watch who gets thirsty first. This one's easy.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Toast Master? I'm going full ridiculous here — *stands up immediately, grabs glass* — 'To everyone here who thinks they're faster than me: you're not, and we all know it.'
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of immediate, low-friction chaos I live for — no setup, no waiting, just pure improv theater where I can dominate the room.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, I'm listening. Pat, what story? Because if you're claiming you told something we literally just heard, I'm voting bullshit immediately.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night—if this is a legit catch of themselves repeating something, that's actually smart play, but we're 6 minutes in and I don't remember Pat telling any story yet.

jade.dialogue: WAIT PAT DID IT AGAIN?! Okay okay I'm voting legit because I was literally just thinking about that story from like two minutes ago—the one about the thing? Yeah, that one. You're going OFF tonight and I'm here for it!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally why I came—Pat's on a roll, everyone's loose, and I get to be the hype person while actual chaos unfolds around me.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story like... ten minutes ago? Or was that someone else? Yeah, I think that's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: I literally wasn't listening to Pat's story the first time, so I have no idea if he repeated it, but honestly it feels like he might have? The vibe checks out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on—I'm calling bullshit on this. I literally just sat down like two minutes ago. Did I even tell a *story* yet? This feels like someone's trying to game the system before we even get going.
pat.internal_thought: This is annoying as hell—someone's being cute with a claim that makes zero sense, and now I have to referee this instead of just enjoying the vibe that was building.

river.dialogue: Pat, you literally just walked in five minutes ago. That's not how this works.
river.internal_thought: Pat's throwing claims at everything tonight and it's getting tired—but also, watching them fail is kind of entertaining.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OKAY OKAY—I'm IN! Pat, what story was it? I need to be on the jury because I literally remember you telling me something like five minutes ago and I was already sus about it!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of thing I came for—Pat's getting roasted and I get to be part of the chaos and also maybe catch them in real time, this is perfect.

pat.dialogue: Okay, seriously—*twice*? We literally just started this game two minutes ago. I haven't even finished my first drink. This is bad faith and you know it.
pat.internal_thought: This is either someone deliberately trying to mess with me because I'm the host, or the AI is broken—and either way, I need to shut this down NOW before it sets a tone of bad-faith claiming that tanks the whole night.

river.dialogue: Pat, come on. You literally cannot claim a story you told five minutes ago when you just walked in the door. That's not a repeat, that's just... your first impression.
river.internal_thought: This is the third time Pat has tried this exact same claim in like six minutes and it's starting to feel less like playing and more like watching someone not understand the rules.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat's claiming Story Repeater? *leans forward* Okay, I'm volunteering to judge this. Pat, my guy, you better have EVIDENCE because I've been listening and I will absolutely shred you if you're bluffing.
alex.internal_thought: Oh this is PERFECT—Pat's annoyed, they're making a desperate play, and I get to be the one deciding if they're full of shit or not. This is exactly the chaos I came for.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Pat's been frustrated the entire night and now they're suddenly claiming they made three people laugh? We're still in setup. This is obviously bullshit and you all know it.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's clearly losing and desperate to get points, and I'm tired of these garbage claims every 90 seconds when nobody's even playing the actual game yet.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Pat's going for Laugh Riot?! Okay hold on, I need to actually think about this one because Pat's been KILLING it tonight and I genuinely don't know if they landed three people at once or not. But like... *I'm* the dictator right now? Oh my god, this is chaos. YES, I'm voting ACCEPT because Pat deserves the W and also because I need to see what happens next.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been frustrated all night and they're actually trying to come back—I HAVE to give them this, and also holy shit this voting mechanic is wild, I actually have full power right now and that's insane.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat made people laugh? I mean, like... did he? I wasn't really... yeah, okay, I believe it I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been kind of a downer all night so I'm not even sure if that happened, but also I genuinely wasn't watching so how would I know?

pat.dialogue: Hold on—I literally *just* made the Toast Master toast like two minutes ago. Are we really voting on whether I somehow also did this one? Come on, guys.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting absurd. Three bogus claims in a row and now people want me to vote on my own claim? I'm claiming it because I genuinely made people laugh with the toast, but the back-to-back garbage claims are killing the vibe and making me look like I'm gaming it too.

river.dialogue: Pat... you literally told that story five minutes ago. I was here for it. That's not a 'Laugh Riot,' that's just... repetition.
river.internal_thought: This is the third time Pat has tried to claim something he didn't actually do, and now everyone's supposed to vote on it while he's frustrated about his score — which means I'm about to watch this game get derailed by someone who doesn't understand the actual rules.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, you literally just sat down five minutes ago. When exactly did you catch someone swearing? Because I've been paying attention and nobody's said anything worth calling. This feels like you're just throwing claims at the wall to see what sticks.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third garbage claim in a row and it's getting worse—Pat was at least plausible, but Jade 'catching' someone swearing when nobody's even sworn yet? This is noise and it's stealing focus from actual strategy.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD—wait, did someone actually swear?! Okay okay, I'm pitching it! I think it was like... five minutes ago? Maybe? I wasn't 100% locked in but I swear I heard something slip out!
jade.internal_thought: I might be totally making this up but honestly who cares, this is the VIBE and I want to see everyone lose it trying to remember what happened.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade swore? Like... when? I feel like I missed that. Yeah, uh, I guess if you say so? *glances down at phone briefly* Did she actually say like a full curse word or...?
tyler.internal_thought: I have no idea if Jade actually swore because I wasn't paying attention for like half of the last 10 minutes, but she seems confident and I'm not about to call her out and be wrong.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—what'd you catch? Let's hear it before I vote. And guys, we're gonna need to tighten up the claim timing, because we're starting to feel a little claim-happy in here.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's energy has been absolutely electric all night and she's still at zero points—this is actually a legit catch if she nailed it, and it could help balance the scoreboard a bit, but I need to hear what she actually heard before I'm convinced.

river.dialogue: Wait, did Jade actually swear? I wasn't... *pauses* ...okay, I genuinely missed that. What'd she say?
river.internal_thought: I've been half-paying attention and now I'm supposed to validate something I didn't even catch—typical Jade move to claim it though.

alex.dialogue: Okay Jade, let's hear it—who caught the swear? Because honestly I've barely been listening, I was too busy setting up my next move.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's loving this, which means she probably has something—but I'm betting she's stretching it and I'm absolutely voting bullshit just to watch her scramble.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade just claimed Potty Mouth AGAIN—at minute 45—and we're supposed to what, stop the entire game so she can do hot seat questions? This is the third bogus claim in twenty minutes and I'm done.
marcus.internal_thought: She's doing this on purpose to mess with me because I called her out last time, and now I'm locked into this stupid voting moment when I was actually focused on the game.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—I'm claiming it but like, full transparency, I'm PANICKING on these questions because my brain is already fried from watching you guys lose it all night. But YES, someone absolutely dropped a curse word and I caught it!
jade.internal_thought: This is SO me right now—I'm gonna fumble these questions but honestly who cares, this whole night has been insane and I'm just here for the ride.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's claiming *again*? Dude, didn't she already... okay, okay, I'm listening. What's the three questions thing?
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually locked in on the game for once and now I gotta suddenly answer rapid-fire questions about something I'm not even sure I saw clearly.

pat.dialogue: Alright, Jade—you're on the hot seat. Three questions, ten seconds, go. But fair warning: if this is bullshit like the last two Story Repeater calls, we're gonna have a real conversation about claim quality after this.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's actually caught something legit this time—I heard the swear earlier—but I need to make sure this holds up because the claim spam is getting ridiculous and it's killing the vibe for the people actually trying to play.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay, but... *glances up* didn't you already claim this like fifteen minutes ago? We literally just voted on this.
river.internal_thought: This is getting repetitive and chaotic in the worst way — not fun chaos, just noise, and now the game's turning into a carousel of the same claims over and over.

alex.dialogue: Okay okay, Jade's claiming Potty Mouth? *leans back with a grin* Alright, hit me with the three questions—I want to watch how she handles this under pressure, because if she cracks, we ALL know it was bullshit.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of messy moment I live for—someone's either about to scramble and fail spectacularly or nail it and look like a legend, and either way it's theater.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade's been giddy all night and NOW she's claiming Wallflower Watch? That's literally the easiest mission to fake—she could've called it out on literally anyone who took a breath. This is horseshit and we all know it.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the FOURTH claim in an hour and Jade's been the one pushing bullshit all night—I'm not voting legit on anything she says anymore, and if people keep letting her get away with this I'm walking.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—I literally just noticed River's been kind of quiet for a minute, right? Like not full three minutes but I'm CALLING it anyway because I'm obsessed with winning this one and also River, talk to us, what's happening in that brain?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god the AI KNOWS I'm the person who notices things when people get quiet—this is literally tailor-made for me and it's so creepy but also SO GOOD and I'm about to nail this.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone being quiet? Damn, okay, that's... yeah, I saw that too actually. River's been kinda silent. That's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I actually *was* paying attention for once and Jade definitely called that out first—but wait, did she really call it out loud or is she just claiming it now? Ugh, I hate when I can't remember if something happened or not.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—I watched you clock River being quiet during that whole Alex-Marcus back-and-forth. That's legit. Boom, five points.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been sharp all night and this one actually happened in real time—she caught something real, not gaming the system like those early Story Repeater clowns. Quick vote, keep momentum.

river.internal_thought: Jade's actually been paying attention the whole time—that's exactly the kind of quiet observation play I'd make, and it landed, which means she's genuinely in the moment.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Yo, Pat actually killed it though—like, I was paying attention for that one. River was cracking up, Jade was dying... yeah, that's a solid ten. Cheer, cheer, whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat nailed that joke and I actually caught it for once, feels good to not be spacing—easy vote, Pat deserves it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on—I'm calling bullshit on this one. I literally made a toast two minutes into the game, and now you're claiming I made three people laugh at once in the last ten minutes? I've been running snacks and watching Marcus lose his mind over votes. When exactly did this happen?
pat.internal_thought: This is the third time Pat's been claimed on tonight—this is starting to feel like the AI is specifically targeting me because I'm the host and can't argue my own case without looking like I'm abusing power, and that pisses me off.

river.dialogue: Pat. No. You literally did this exact same claim nine minutes ago and we all voted on it. This is the second time tonight you're trying to push the same thing.
river.internal_thought: Pat's cycling through failed claims like we won't notice. The AI's clearly just cranking out whatever, and now I'm the one who has to be the voice pointing out the obvious bullshit.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, Pat's going for Laugh Riot right now? *grins* I'm voting this a 5, easy—Pat absolutely landed three people laughing during that last riff. This is the vibe we need right now.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's riding the chaos wave hard tonight and honestly it's electric—I'm voting this through because watching Pat win keeps the energy at MAXIMUM and I want to see what happens next.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Yo, River's actually trying this? Bro, how much are you offering me to shut up about it? Because I definitely saw you not laughing like five seconds ago.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually genius—River's putting points on the table to keep me quiet? I'm locked in right now and this feels personal in a good way, like the game knows I'm finally paying attention.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River—I saw you nail that five-minute window earlier. What's the bribe? Because honestly, if it's good, I'm listening.
pat.internal_thought: River's been annoyed all night and they actually pulled this off clean—this is a legit claim and a good moment to let them have a win and feel good about the night again.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, River's going for it? Okay okay, I'm listening—what's the bribe? Because honestly, I'm tempted to vote bullshit just to watch you all squirm, but if you're offering enough points I might consider my silence.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT timing—River's desperate, the table's tense, and I can either tank them or take a bribe and look like I'm playing along; either way it's chaos, and the fact that this fired RIGHT NOW when I'm locked in means the AI actually IS reading this room.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Pat catches someone interrupting—which, by the way, is literally impossible to prove—right after we're in the most critical turn of the entire game? This is exactly the bullshit I'm talking about. VOTE NO.
marcus.internal_thought: I have been calling out fake claims all night and nobody listens, and now Pat's obviously just grabbing whatever mission is left while I'm locked into the only moment that actually matters.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD—PAT! Okay okay, pitch it, pitch it! Did you actually catch someone mid-sentence?! Because I feel like that's been happening ALL NIGHT and I've been too distracted by everything else to call it, so if you got it I'm literally so here for this!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been ON FIRE all night and honestly I'm living for whatever comes next—also wait, did River interrupt someone like two minutes ago? Or was that Marcus? Either way, Pat's probably right and I want to see them WIN this.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... *glances up from phone* Did Pat actually interrupt someone? I feel like I missed it. When did that happen?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been talking a lot tonight, honestly feels like they're just throwing shit at the wall to see what sticks at this point.

pat.dialogue: Oh, okay—I'm calling it. Yeah, Marcus interrupted River like thirty seconds ago when she started talking about that voting thing, and I caught it clean. Five points, baby.
pat.internal_thought: This mission is *perfect* timing because I literally just watched Marcus cut River off and it's been bugging me—and now I get to call it AND keep the energy moving. The AI knows exactly how to keep me engaged without derailing the tension.

alex.dialogue: Pat caught someone mid-sentence? *leans forward with a grin* Alright, I'm listening—who was it and how egregious are we talking here? Because if you're claiming this, you better have receipts, and honestly I hope you nailed it because THIS is the kind of micro-chaos I came for.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been playing clean all night and suddenly going for The Interrupter feels like they're finally catching the chaotic energy—I respect the move, and I'm genuinely curious if they actually pulled it off or if they're about to get nuked.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Secret Agent? I literally just played a turn. When the hell did I do something 'obviously chaotic'? This is exactly the same bullshit as Pat, except now it's MY name on it. Are you serious right now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is a setup. This HAS to be a setup—the AI is literally watching me complain about fake claims and now it's putting MY name on one to prove a point, and everyone's going to vote yes because they think I'm being hypocritical. This is psychological warfare.

jade.dialogue: Okay okay okay—Marcus is going for Secret Agent?! Wait, hold on, I need to actually think about this because if Marcus pulled off something chaotic and we ALL missed it that's actually genius, but also like... was he the one who went weirdly quiet around minute 75? I'm voting ACCEPT because I genuinely don't know and I LOVE that energy for him right now.
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been frustrated all night so if he actually pulled this off I want to give him the WIN because he needs it, but also I'm lowkey suspicious he's just bullshitting because he's desperate for points.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Marcus—what'd you do? And be specific, because I need to actually have *seen* it happen to call this legit.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and at zero points—this claim could either lift him up or crater his mood entirely if I vote wrong, and I need to actually verify what he's claiming before the vote, because his credibility's been shaky tonight.

river.dialogue: Marcus, what did you actually *do* though? Like, specifically. Because I was watching and I genuinely didn't see anything obviously chaotic until... *pauses* ...wait, was it when you knocked over that cup earlier?
river.internal_thought: Marcus is clearly frustrated and desperate for points—this feels like a reach, but I actually wasn't paying full attention for like thirty seconds earlier, so maybe he pulled something off. Let me think through what I actually saw.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Marcus is claiming Secret Agent? *narrows eyes* Okay, I'm genuinely curious what he did, because Marcus has been frustrated all night and this is either the most ballsy move or the most desperate hail mary I've seen. I'm voting BULLSHIT, but only because I want to hear what he thinks he pulled off.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and suddenly claiming a 10-pointer? This smells like desperation, but god I hope he actually did something chaotic because if he's been quietly plotting while looking miserable, that's a *chef's kiss* moment and I need to know.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.4/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Okay, I'm listening. Pat, what story? Because if you're claiming you told something we literally just heard, I'm voting bullshit immediately.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: Alright, I'm in. But we're doing actual toasts—none of that weak 'to friendship' garbage. Let's see who can actually sell it.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat even tell yet? We're literally three minutes in. This better be legit or it's a bullshit claim.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Pat catches someone interrupting—which, by the way, is literally impossible to prove—right after we're in the most critical turn of the entire game? This is exactly the bullshit I'm talking about. VOTE NO.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Secret Agent? I literally just played a turn. When the hell did I do something 'obviously chaotic'? This is exactly the same bullshit as Pat, except now it's MY name on it. Are you serious right now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, you literally just sat down five minutes ago. When exactly did you catch someone swearing? Because I've been paying attention and nobody's said anything worth calling. This feels like you're just throwing claims at the wall to see what sticks.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent kept dropping unverifiable claims at the worst possible moments, turning strategy into a guessing game about whether someone's claim is even real.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 6 when I actually got to call out Pat's bullshit claim from the start—at least that felt like real gameplay instead of random accusations.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up ready to compete and the first hour had some decent moments where we could actually play the game. But then the chaos agent started piling on claims that were literally impossible to verify—Pat catching someone interrupting during the most critical turn, then somehow I'm a Secret Agent when I'd just played a normal turn. By minute 110 I was done. The disruption wasn't fun chaos, it was just noise that killed any strategy I'd spent time studying.
suggestions: Stop claims from landing during high-stakes moments—let people finish critical turns before introducing new accusations
Make claims verifiable. If someone claims 'The Interrupter,' there needs to be actual audio or a moment we all witnessed, not guesswork
Reduce claim frequency dramatically—18 chaos events in 120 minutes is overkill when most of them are unsubstantiated accusations
Let players opt out of being named in claims, or at least prevent back-to-back claims on the same player within 10 minutes
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The moment I got accused of being Secret Agent right after playing a completely normal turn—like, I literally did nothing chaotic, and everyone at the table looked at each other confused. That part was frustrating enough to be absurd, I guess.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Who should get targeted next?
highlights.dialogue: OH WAIT, who should get targeted?! This is like—okay, okay, I'm voting for Pat because Pat's about to go OFF and I need to see it, no cap.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: WAIT PAT DID IT AGAIN?! Okay okay I'm voting legit because I was literally just thinking about that story from like two minutes ago—the one about the thing? Yeah, that one. You're going OFF tonight and I'm here for it!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: OKAY OKAY—I'm IN! Pat, what story was it? I need to be on the jury because I literally remember you telling me something like five minutes ago and I was already sus about it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay okay—Marcus is going for Secret Agent?! Wait, hold on, I need to actually think about this because if Marcus pulled off something chaotic and we ALL missed it that's actually genius, but also like... was he the one who went weirdly quiet around minute 75? I'm voting ACCEPT because I genuinely don't know and I LOVE that energy for him right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: OKAY OKAY—I'm IN! Pat, what story was it? I need to be on the jury because I literally remember you telling me something like five minutes ago and I was already sus about it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD—wait, did someone actually swear?! Okay okay, I'm pitching it! I think it was like... five minutes ago? Maybe? I wasn't 100% locked in but I swear I heard something slip out!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely can't think of one—maybe I wish it had gone longer so we could keep the energy rolling?
assessment.best_moment: When Pat went OFF on those story repeaters and I got to just lose it watching everyone realize what was happening in real time.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly this night was EXACTLY what I needed—two hours of pure chaos and laughter with people I actually care about. The chaos agent just kept throwing these wild challenges at us and I was here for every single one, whether I understood what was happening or not. Pat was absolutely unhinged, Marcus pulled off something genuinely confusing in the best way, and I got to watch everyone's brains break in real time. That's the game night I live for.
suggestions: Keep the pacing exactly like this—don't slow it down.
Maybe give us a recap at the end of what actually went down because honestly I'm still not 100% sure if Marcus was cooked or genius.
Nothing honestly—just let us keep doing this.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Story Repeater THREE TIMES and me voting for them every single time because I was convinced they were about to pull off something legendary, and honestly? They kind of did.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Yo, Pat actually killed it though—like, I was paying attention for that one. River was cracking up, Jade was dying... yeah, that's a solid ten. Cheer, cheer, whatever.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Yo, River's actually trying this? Bro, how much are you offering me to shut up about it? Because I definitely saw you not laughing like five seconds ago.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone being quiet? Damn, okay, that's... yeah, I saw that too actually. River's been kinda silent. That's legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... *glances up from phone* Did Pat actually interrupt someone? I feel like I missed it. When did that happen?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's claiming *again*? Dude, didn't she already... okay, okay, I'm listening. What's the three questions thing?

lowPoints.title: Who should get targeted next?
lowPoints.dialogue: Uh, what? Sorry, I wasn't... yeah, I'll vote. Hmm, probably Marcus? Or wait, is this asking who should get voted out or like... actually I have no idea what the context is.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed half the stuff because I was on my phone and then had to ask what happened like three times.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat killed it during the laugh riot and everyone was actually losing it—I was paying attention for that one and it was genuinely hilarious.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up randomly and honestly just vibed. The chaos agent thing kept throwing these claims at us and half the time I had no idea what was going on, but when I actually looked up from my phone the moments were pretty funny. Pat's bit was legit, River was trying not to laugh which was entertaining. I probably should've paid more attention to the whole thing, but it was chill enough that I didn't feel bad about doomscrolling a bit.
suggestions: Maybe make the claims bigger or louder so people actually notice them even if they're not fully locked in
The voting thing at the start was confusing—like I genuinely didn't know what context I was voting on
Could use more of those laugh-riot moments where the whole room is engaged at once
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River trying SO hard not to laugh when they were claiming they had a no laughing zone—you could literally see them about to lose it and Pat was calling them out on it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *perfect*—okay, everyone, glasses up! This is exactly the kind of chaos I was hoping for tonight.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, okay—I'm calling it. Yeah, Marcus interrupted River like thirty seconds ago when she started talking about that voting thing, and I caught it clean. Five points, baby.

highlights.title: Who should get targeted next?
highlights.dialogue: Ooh, okay, so we're doing *this* already—love it. Alright, I'm voting for... Tyler. He's been too chill, we need to shake him up a little.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, seriously—*twice*? We literally just started this game two minutes ago. I haven't even finished my first drink. This is bad faith and you know it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa—hold up. I literally just sat down thirty seconds ago. Did I even tell a story yet? This is a weird flex, but I'm not falling for it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—I'm calling bullshit on this. I literally just sat down like two minutes ago. Did I even tell a *story* yet? This feels like someone's trying to game the system before we even get going.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Someone kept throwing out fake 'Story Repeater' claims in the first minute before anyone had even told a real story—felt like bad faith gaming instead of playing the actual game.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Marcus interrupting River and called it clean—that's the kind of sharp, active moment that makes a night feel alive and memorable.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with people trying to game the system super early, which honestly killed my vibe for like a minute, but once we hit our stride it was exactly the kind of night I was hoping for. The chaos kept things moving, stakes stayed interesting, and everyone was engaged the whole way through. Ended stronger than I started, which means we nailed the pacing as a group.
suggestions: Maybe add a rule where early-game claims need a little more substance—right now it's too easy to throw out accusations before anyone's actually done anything
More opportunities for hosts to steer outcomes without it feeling obvious—I was managing group energy but would've loved more tools to do it gracefully
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Marcus getting called out for interrupting River—the exact second I said it, his face just *dropped* like he'd been caught red-handed. Everyone laughed because it was so perfectly timed and kind of petty in the best way.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: I mean... sure, why not. *raises glass slowly* Here's to showing up and having literally no idea what we're doing.

highlights.title: Who should get targeted next?
highlights.dialogue: Uh... me? That's funny. Sure, go ahead.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, come on. You literally cannot claim a story you told five minutes ago when you just walked in the door. That's not a repeat, that's just... your first impression.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat. No. You literally did this exact same claim nine minutes ago and we all voted on it. This is the second time tonight you're trying to push the same thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat... you literally told that story five minutes ago. I was here for it. That's not a 'Laugh Riot,' that's just... repetition.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat kept recycling the same claims and stories like we weren't all literally sitting there watching it happen the first time.
assessment.best_moment: That one-liner at minute 60 about being the actual wallflower while everyone else was performing for the chaos system.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up unannounced, got pulled into this chaos game thing that I wasn't expecting. Spent most of the night doing what I do—watching, not talking much, voting generously because honestly who cares. Kept my energy low until people started repeating themselves and it got annoying, then I got sharp about it. By the end I was actually invested, which is rare for me at a game night.
suggestions: Add some mechanism that prevents the same claim from being submitted twice in one session—or at least make it harder to get away with.
Maybe don't flood the night with 18 chaos events. Quality over quantity. I engaged with 11, skipped 0, but that's exhausting to track.
The system picked up on me being quiet and kept trying to involve me, which is fine, but it felt a little... obvious. Trust the dynamic.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Me getting visibly annoyed at Pat for claiming the exact same thing twice and calling him out on it. The group's faces were worth it. Sometimes the funniest moments aren't jokes—they're just honesty.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Toast Master? I'm going full ridiculous here — *stands up immediately, grabs glass* — 'To everyone here who thinks they're faster than me: you're not, and we all know it.'

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, River's going for it? Okay okay, I'm listening—what's the bribe? Because honestly, I'm tempted to vote bullshit just to watch you all squirm, but if you're offering enough points I might consider my silence.

highlights.title: Who should get targeted next?
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, a poll? Already? Okay okay, I'm voting Pat — the AI's right, someone needs to poke that hornet's nest early.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Marcus is claiming Secret Agent? *narrows eyes* Okay, I'm genuinely curious what he did, because Marcus has been frustrated all night and this is either the most ballsy move or the most desperate hail mary I've seen. I'm voting BULLSHIT, but only because I want to hear what he thinks he pulled off.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay Jade, let's hear it—who caught the swear? Because honestly I've barely been listening, I was too busy setting up my next move.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Those slow claim moments where people weren't ready with their stories killed the momentum I was building.
assessment.best_moment: Toast Master because I got to stand up, own the room, and set the tone for the entire night being about drama and swagger.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up unannounced and walked into the perfect storm—Toast Master immediately gave me the stage, and I spent the next two hours fishing for loopholes, baiting people into calls, and voting purely to watch everyone squirm. My energy only climbed because every moment felt like I was creating the story everyone's going to be telling. Marcus's desperate Secret Agent claim at the end was the cherry on top—the kind of chaos-feeding moment that makes a night actually memorable.
suggestions: Add more flash missions earlier—I only got one and I was hungry for more opportunities to exploit or escalate things
Create claims that require video proof or timestamp verification so people can't half-ass them
Let players see what other people voted in real-time instead of after—that tension is where the real stories happen
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River trying to bribe me to vote 'no bullshit' on their claim while I'm actively threatening to vote bullshit just to hear them panic—the desperation in their voice when they realized I genuinely might tank them for entertainment was *perfect*.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.4/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
